title: Tetrastigma laevigatum
intro: Tetrastigma laevigatum, also known as the "five-angled grape vine" or "Chinese grapevine", is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae, native to southern China. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Tetrastigma laevigatum include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory disorders.
